title: The University of Sydney: AI Policy Crawl Report
summary: The University of Sydney has a comprehensive and sector-leading AI policy framework. Key findings: Academic Integrity Policy 2022 explicitly addresses AI misuse as both an integrity breach and potential contract cheating. From Semester 1 2025, the default was reversed to allow AI use for non-examination assessments unless prohibited by the coordinator. The University has adopted a two-lane approach (secure Lane 1 and open Lane 2) from Semester 2 2025. All students receive free Microsoft Copilot for Web with protected mode via UniKey. Published AI guardrails prohibit entering confidential or sensitive data into AI tools. Turnitin AI detection may be used but is not the sole evidence for integrity cases. AI use in assessments must be acknowledged or students face breach proceedings. Assessment Procedures require coordinators to specify AI rules per assessment. All claims are agent_reviewed only, backed by 8 verified official sources from sydney.edu.au and educational-innovation.sydney.edu.au.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
